front: What is the formal definition of an equivalence relation on a set A?
back: An equivalence relation on a set A is a relation R that is reflexive (aRa for all a in A), symmetric (if aRb then bRa), and transitive (if aRb and bRc then aRc).

front: What is the equivalence class of an element a under an equivalence relation R?
back: The equivalence class of a, denoted [a], is the set of all elements in A that are related to a. Formally, [a] = {b ∈ A | (a, b) ∈ R}.

front: What is the fundamental connection between equivalence relations and partitions?
back: Every equivalence relation on a set A induces a partition of A (its set of distinct equivalence classes). Conversely, every partition of A induces an equivalence relation (where elements are related if they belong to the same partition block).

front: What is the quotient set A/R?
back: The quotient set of A modulo R is the set of all equivalence classes of A under R. It is the partition of A induced by the equivalence relation R.

front: What is the Kernel Relation of a function f: A → B and why is it important?
back: The kernel relation of f is defined by a₁ ~ a₂ if and only if f(a₁) = f(a₂). This relation is always an equivalence relation on A, and it demonstrates how functions naturally induce partitions (on their domain) through their fibers (pre-images of points).
